data.meta_description: Celebrate Virgen del Carmen with a yacht charter in Marbella. Explore Puerto Banús, enjoy 12.5m boats from €749, and honour the patron saint of fishermen.
data.summary: The Virgen del Carmen festival in Marbella combines maritime tradition with luxury yacht charters. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús, offering a unique way to experience the procession and coastal celebrations.
key_facts: Virgen del Carmen is celebrated on 16 July, honouring the patron saint of fishermen.
Marbella's procession features boats decorated with flowers and flags, sailing from Puerto Banús.
Our yacht charters start at €749 for 2 hours (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

faq.q: What is the Virgen del Carmen festival in Marbella?
faq.a: The Virgen del Carmen festival, held on 16 July, is a maritime celebration honouring the patron saint of fishermen. In Marbella, it includes a boat procession from Puerto Banús, where decorated vessels carry the statue of the Virgin out to sea, followed by festivities along the coast.

faq.q: Can I charter a yacht to watch the Virgen del Carmen procession?
faq.a: Yes, chartering a yacht from Puerto Banús is a popular way to view the procession. Our boats offer prime positions on the water, with licensed skippers handling navigation so you can focus on the spectacle. Book early as availability is limited.

faq.q: Which yachts are best for the Virgen del Carmen event?
faq.a: For a group of up to 11, the Azimut 39 (€749/2h) is ideal for short trips. Larger parties (up to 12) can opt for the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, includes a jet ski). All depart from Puerto Banús, close to the procession route.

faq.q: What is included in a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). You just need to bring your group and enjoy the tradition.

faq.q: How much does a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, the minimum is 4 hours at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp. All rates include the items listed above.

faq.q: Where do the Virgen del Carmen charters depart from?
faq.a: All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. This is also where the festival's main maritime procession begins, making it the most convenient location for joining the celebrations.

faq.q: Do I need to book in advance for the Virgen del Carmen?
faq.a: Yes, early booking is strongly recommended. The festival is one of Marbella's busiest nautical events, and our fleet books out quickly. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability and secure your preferred yacht.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or decorations for the Virgen del Carmen?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food or decorations (such as flowers or flags) for the occasion. Our skippers can advise on what works best on board. Just ensure decorations are secure and do not interfere with safety equipment.

data.body_html:
Every 16 July, Marbella's coastline transforms into a floating cathedral as fishing boats, yachts, and leisure craft gather to honour the Virgen del Carmen, patron saint of seafarers. The tradition is deeply rooted in Spain's maritime culture, and a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter Marbella offers front-row seats to the spectacle. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet provides a practical and memorable way to join the procession and celebrate along the Costa del Sol.

What Is the Virgen del Carmen Festival?

The Virgen del Carmen festival dates back centuries, celebrating the Virgin as protector of fishermen and sailors. In Marbella, the day begins with a mass at the church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación, followed by a land procession to the port. The statue of the Virgin is then carried onto a decorated boat and escorted by a flotilla of vessels out to sea. The route typically passes Marbella Marina, Cabopino, and the Golden Mile, with participants throwing flower petals into the water as a tribute.

Why Charter a Yacht for Virgen del Carmen?

Watching from shore means fighting crowds and limited views. A private charter puts you on the water, close to the action, with the freedom to follow the procession at your own pace. Our boats come with a licensed skipper, so you can relax with a glass of cava while the crew handles navigation. The experience combines cultural tradition with the luxury of a day on the Mediterranean.

Our Fleet for the Festival

All our yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the epicentre of Marbella's nautical life. For a short, affordable outing, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 passengers) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 passengers) are excellent choices at €749 for 2 hours. If you're celebrating with a larger group, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 passengers) offers a minimum 4-hour charter for €4,719, which includes a jet ski. Other boats, such as the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86, are available on request via WhatsApp. Every charter includes skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% VAT.

What to Expect on the Day

The procession typically starts in the late afternoon, with boats gathering at Puerto Banús. The atmosphere is festive: music, cheers, and the scent of salt air mix with the sound of horns. You'll see fishing vessels draped with ribbons and flowers, while larger yachts like our Mangusta 80 provide a comfortable vantage point. After the procession, many charters continue along the coast to Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a swim and sunset drinks.

How to Book Your Virgen del Carmen Yacht Charter

Demand for 16 July is high, so early booking is essential. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability for your preferred yacht. We recommend confirming at least two weeks in advance. For groups of up to 12, the Azimut 58 Flybridge or Mangusta 80 are popular choices. If you're planning a larger celebration, inquire about the Ferretti 94 or Canados 86 (both 12 passengers, pricing on request).

Other Coastal Events Around Virgen del Carmen

While Marbella's celebration is the highlight, nearby towns like Estepona and Sotogrande also host processions on the same day. Combining a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter Marbella with a visit to these locations is possible with a full-day booking. Our skippers know the best routes and can adjust the itinerary to include multiple viewpoints.

Practical Tips for Your Charter

  • Arrive early: Puerto Banús gets busy on 16 July. Plan to board at least 30 minutes before departure.
  • Dress comfortably: Light clothing, sunscreen, and a hat are essential. Evenings can be cooler, so bring a light jacket.
  • Bring a camera: The colourful procession against the backdrop of La Concha mountain is a photographer's dream.
  • Respect the tradition: The Virgen del Carmen is a religious event. Keep noise levels appropriate during the religious moments.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Virgen del Carmen festival in Marbella?

The Virgen del Carmen festival, held on 16 July, is a maritime celebration honouring the patron saint of fishermen. In Marbella, it includes a boat procession from Puerto Banús, where decorated vessels carry the statue of the Virgin out to sea, followed by festivities along the coast.

Can I charter a yacht to watch the Virgen del Carmen procession?

Yes, chartering a yacht from Puerto Banús is a popular way to view the procession. Our boats offer prime positions on the water, with licensed skippers handling navigation so you can focus on the spectacle. Book early as availability is limited.

Which yachts are best for the Virgen del Carmen event?

For a group of up to 11, the Azimut 39 (€749/2h) is ideal for short trips. Larger parties (up to 12) can opt for the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, includes a jet ski). All depart from Puerto Banús, close to the procession route.

What is included in a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish VAT (21%). You just need to bring your group and enjoy the tradition.

How much does a Virgen del Carmen yacht charter cost in Marbella?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39. For larger yachts like the Mangusta 80, the minimum is 4 hours at €4,719. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp. All rates include the items listed above.

Where do the Virgen del Carmen charters depart from?

All our charters depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. This is also where the festival's main maritime procession begins, making it the most convenient location for joining the celebrations.

Do I need to book in advance for the Virgen del Carmen?

Yes, early booking is strongly recommended. The festival is one of Marbella's busiest nautical events, and our fleet books out quickly. Contact us via WhatsApp to check availability and secure your preferred yacht.

Can I bring my own food or decorations for the Virgen del Carmen?

Yes, you are welcome to bring additional food or decorations (such as flowers or flags) for the occasion. Our skippers can advise on what works best on board. Just ensure decorations are secure and do not interfere with safety equipment.
